title: CEO pitch + One-Hundred-Years-of-Solitude + DMV + start OSIG (March 3 2022 stacked todo)
html:
TL;DR

  • March 3, 2022 stacked todo with "-CEO pitch" as the first line — Yahir prepping for what was likely a FBLA-style or class CEO-pitch presentation (review slides, box dimensions, etc.).
  • Same note simultaneously logs: "start OSIG process" — the earliest dated trace in the vault of Yahir actually beginning to apply to OSIG (Oregon State Investment Group); call DMV for license; pick up One Hundred Years of Solitude; check CS schedule, "likely change calc"; "hash out financial situation with JCEF".
  • The note dates the OSIG origin moment to early March 2022, two weeks before the housing scramble and ~7 weeks before the self curriculum workbook proper.
  • Captures the CS-pivot in flight — "likely change calc" and "look in CS schedule" lines confirm the major change was active in March, matching 2022 03 31 list for web back home's tuition-cleanup work.
  • The same note also picks up the one hundred years of solitude reading (which gets its own reading log eight days later) — Yahir's parallel CS / business / literary tracks running in one stacked todo.

Key fragments

"-CEO pitch / -review other slides / -box dimensions"

"-get hundred years in solitude / -call dmv for license / -start OSIG process"

"-look in CS schedule / -likely change calc"

"-hash out financial situation with jcef"

Why it matters

This is the OSIG-application-day note. If you want to pin a date on when Yahir began the OSIG application process, it's March 3, 2022. The note also confirms the funding fragility: in the same line, he's talking to JCEF (Jackson County Education Fund) about money — i.e., the bridge income reality is operative even at the start of the OSIG arc, not added later.

Open questions

  • Whose "CEO pitch" — a class? An FBLA holdover? A Beaver Hatchery / Beaver Tank pitch?
  • What was the "box dimensions" line referring to — physical product mockup?
  • Did the "likely change calc" decision get made? (Calc 1 vs Calc 2 vs intro-CS-math.)
